# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    parameters {
        string(name: 'ARTIFACT_URL', defaultValue: 'https://example.com/path/to/my-app.zip', description: 'URL of the artifact to download (e.g., S3 pre-signed URL, Artifactory direct link, HTTP endpoint)')
        string(name: 'ARTIFACT_FILENAME', defaultValue: 'my-app.zip', description: 'The filename the artifact will be saved as locally and on the server (e.g., my-app.zip, service.tar.gz)')
        string(name: 'PRODUCTION_SERVER_HOST', defaultValue: 'prod.example.com', description: 'Hostname or IP address of the production server')
        string(name: 'PRODUCTION_SERVER_USER', defaultValue: 'deployuser', description: 'Username to SSH into the production server')
        string(name: 'PRODUCTION_SERVER_CREDENTIALS_ID', defaultValue: 'prod-ssh-key', description: 'Jenkins Credentials ID for SSH access to the production server (must be of type "SSH Username with private key")')
        string(name: 'TARGET_DIR_ON_SERVER', defaultValue: '/opt/my-app', description: 'The absolute base directory on the production server where the artifact will be deployed')
        booleanParam(name: 'UNPACK_ARTIFACT', defaultValue: true, description: 'If true, the artifact will be unpacked on the server (assumes .zip, .tar.gz, or .tar)')
        string(name: 'SERVICE_NAME', defaultValue: 'my-app', description: 'Name of the systemd service (e.g., my-app.service) to restart after deployment (leave blank to skip restart)')
    }

    stages {
        stage('Download Artifact') {
            steps {
                script {
                    echo "Downloading artifact from ${params.ARTIFACT_URL}..."
                    // -L: follow redirects, -f: fail silently on HTTP errors, -o: write to file
                    sh "curl -L -f -o ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME} \"${params.ARTIFACT_URL}\""
                    echo "Artifact '${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}' downloaded successfully."
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Deploy Artifact') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def remoteArtifactPath = "${params.TARGET_DIR_ON_SERVER}/${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}"
                    echo "Deploying '${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}' to ${params.PRODUCTION_SERVER_USER}@${params.PRODUCTION_SERVER_HOST}:${remoteArtifactPath}"

                    // Use sshagent to provide SSH credentials for scp and ssh commands
                    // Requires the "SSH Agent Plugin" to be installed in Jenkins
                    sshagent(credentials: [params.PRODUCTION_SERVER_CREDENTIALS_ID]) {
                        def sshCommand = "ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null ${params.PRODUCTION_SERVER_USER}@${params.PRODUCTION_SERVER_HOST}"

                        // 1. Ensure the target directory exists on the server
                        sh "${sshCommand} \"mkdir -p ${params.TARGET_DIR_ON_SERVER}\""
                        echo "Target directory '${params.TARGET_DIR_ON_SERVER}' ensured on server."

                        // 2. Copy the artifact to the production server
                        sh "scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME} ${params.PRODUCTION_SERVER_USER}@${params.PRODUCTION_SERVER_HOST}:${remoteArtifactPath}"
                        echo "Artifact copied to production server."

                        // 3. Unpack the artifact if enabled
                        if (params.UNPACK_ARTIFACT) {
                            echo "Attempting to unpack artifact on server..."
                            if (params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME.endsWith('.zip')) {
                                sh "${sshCommand} \"cd ${params.TARGET_DIR_ON_SERVER} && unzip -o
